headline: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
summary: The Atlantic developed a public, searchable database of music used for AI training. Two datasets contain 12 million and 9 million tracks respectively. This resource helps reveal how AI models are being trained on existing music.

headline: Musician and YouTuber Hainbach on ‘Breath of the Wild’ and Swiss Army Knives
summary: Experimental musician Hainbach, known for using lab equipment, discusses his unique approach to music. He likens his methods to the "Dark Souls of synthesis." Hainbach also shares insights on "Breath of the Wild" and versatility.

headline: Moves of the Diamond Hand is an unfinished, irresistibly weird dice-based RPG
summary: "Moves of the Diamond Hand" is a creative and unconventional dice-based RPG. Players engage in strange conversations and frequent dice rolls. Despite being unfinished, the game offers a uniquely rewarding experience.

headline: Toy Story has the right take on tech
summary: The article discusses "Toy Story's" perspective on technology, reflecting current tech discussions. It's part of the Installer newsletter, covering "Verge-iest stuff." The piece links to broader conversations around figures like Sam Bankman-Fried.

headline: The Best Deals Today: Save on Donkey Kong Country Returns HD, Splatoon Raiders, Tokyo Ghoul Box Set, and More
summary: IGN features today's top gaming and entertainment deals. Discounts are available on Donkey Kong Country Returns HD. Save on Splatoon Raiders and the Tokyo Ghoul Box Set.

headline: Cheers Co-Creator James Burrows Dies at 85
summary: James Burrows, co-creator of the iconic show "Cheers," has passed away. He was also a director for the hit series "Friends." Burrows died at the age of 85.

headline: Halo: Campaign Evolved Split-Screen Co-Op on PS5 Requires PlayStation Plus for Both Players
summary: Halo: Campaign Evolved split-screen co-op launches next month for PS5. Both players will need active PlayStation Plus subscriptions. This requirement ensures online access for local co-op on the console.

headline: Ubisoft Co-Founder Claude Guillemot Dead at 69 After Plane Crash in France
summary: Claude Guillemot, a co-founder of Ubisoft, has died. He was 69 years old and perished in a plane crash in France. Ubisoft is known for publishing the "Assassin's Creed" series.

headline: Nine people in critical condition among 100 injured in fatal Bedford train crash
summary: A fatal train crash in Bedford has left one driver dead. Over 100 people were injured, with nine in critical condition. British Transport Police are actively investigating the incident.

headline: Talk of Starmer staying on to fight is fading - fast
summary: Support for Prime Minister Starmer is rapidly diminishing. This comes after Andy Burnham's by-election victory. Laura Kuenssberg observes the swift evaporation of Starmer's political momentum.

headline: King Charles to reveal personal tax bill for first time as monarch
summary: King Charles will disclose his personal tax bill for the first time. The annual financial report for the Royal Family will include this detail. This marks a new level of financial transparency for the monarch.

headline: Extreme heat warning expanded as 37C heatwave approaches UK
summary: An extreme heat warning has been expanded across the UK. Temperatures are expected to reach up to 37C by Tuesday. The Met Office has issued an amber alert for the approaching heatwave.
